pullRequest.title: Changes in semdedup scripts
pullRequest.body:
## Description
<!-- Provide a standalone description of changes in this PR. -->

- Added an if/else block in [extract_dedup_data.py](https://github.com/abdr17/Curator/blob/5200fa971edbbc2df1741a19dbb8bd1813c66df1/nemo_curator/scripts/semdedup/extract_dedup_data.py#L66C5-L66C34) so that it logs `"No semantic duplicates found!"` instead of this warning:

```
UserWarning: Insufficient elements for `head`. 10 elements requested, only 0 elements available. Try passing larger `npartitions` to `head`.
  warnings.warn(
Empty DataFrame
Columns: [cluster, cosine_dist_to_cent, doc_id]
Index: []

```
when duplicates are not found.

- [Changed](https://github.com/abdr17/Curator/blob/5200fa971edbbc2df1741a19dbb8bd1813c66df1/pyproject.toml#L169) the out of date name of the cli command `semdedup_extract_unique_ids` that invokes [extract_dedup_data.py](https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/blob/main/nemo_curator/scripts/semdedup/extract_dedup_data.py) to `semdedup_extract_duplicate_ids`.
- The output of the above command used to be saved at `{config.cache_dir}/{config.clustering_save_loc}/unique_ids_{}.parquet`. This is now [changed](https://github.com/abdr17/Curator/blob/5200fa971edbbc2df1741a19dbb8bd1813c66df1/nemo_curator/modules/semantic_dedup/semanticclusterleveldedup.py#L152) to `{config.cache_dir}/{config.clustering_save_loc}/duplicate_ids_{}.parquet`.
- Changed [README.md](https://github.com/abdr17/Curator/blob/main/nemo_curator/scripts/semdedup/README.md) accordingly.

<!-- Reference any issues closed by this PR with "closes #1234". -->
closes #711

```
semdedup_extract_duplicate_ids --id-column "my_id" --config-file "$CONFIG_FILE"
```

pullRequest.title: Fix failing Trafilatura tests for extracting Chinese and Japanese text

pullRequest.title: Fuse document iterate and extract stages
pullRequest.body:
Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/780.

This change allows the following improvements:

- When tested with limited RAM (`--memory=200g`), the Common Crawl download and extract pipeline without fusion OOM'ed even when scaling all the way down to 16 CPUs. With fusion, the pipeline was able to succeed with 32 CPUs.
- When removing the RAM limit, the pipeline was faster with fusion than without it. See the following benchmark (using https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/blob/main/benchmarking/scripts/common_crawl_benchmark.py).

| Configuration | Runtime |
|-|-|
| Fusion + Ray Data executor | 27m 49.1s |
| No Fusion + Ray Data executor | 29m 33.1s |
| Fusion + Xenna executor | 54m 4.2s |
| No Fusion + Xenna executor | 66m 6.8s |

_Common Crawl benchmarks with `url_limit=16` and `num_cpus=8`_

pullRequest.title: Remove ftfy pin
pullRequest.body:
Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/906. The only part of our codebase that uses ftfy is the `UnicodeReformatter`, which is tested here: https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/blob/main/tests/stages/text/modules/test_modifiers.py. Assuming the test still passes, we should be safe to merge.

Greptile Overview

Updated On: 2025-10-20 21:51:00 UTC ### Greptile Summary This PR removes the version pin on the `ftfy` dependency, changing it from `ftfy==6.1.1` to `ftfy` in the `pyproject.toml` file. The change addresses issue #906 and allows the package manager to install any compatible version of ftfy rather than being locked to version 6.1.1. Within the NeMo Curator codebase, ftfy is exclusively used by the `UnicodeReformatter` module for text cleaning operations, which has existing test coverage in `tests/stages/text/modules/test_modifiers.py`. This change aligns with best practices for dependency management by relying on semantic versioning rather than hard pins, giving users more flexibility while still maintaining compatibility through ftfy's API stability. ### Important Files Changed
Changed Files | Filename | Score | Overview | |----------|-------|----------| | pyproject.toml | 4/5 | Removed version pin from ftfy dependency (changed from `ftfy==6.1.1` to `ftfy`) in the text_cpu optional dependencies |
### Confidence score: 4/5 - This PR is safe to merge with low risk, provided existing tests pass - Score reflects minimal change scope (single dependency pin removal) and existing test coverage for the affected module (`UnicodeReformatter`), though slightly lowered because the PR depends on runtime validation rather than compile-time guarantees that newer ftfy versions maintain API compatibility - No files require special attention; the change is straightforward and limited to dependency management

pullRequest.title: Check codebase for typos and fix identified issues
pullRequest.body:
This PR addresses a comprehensive typo check across the NeMo Curator codebase before release. Through systematic analysis using spell checking tools and pattern matching, several typos were identified and corrected.

## Changes Made

### Documentation Fixes
- **docs/reference/index.md**: Fixed "Qucklinks" → "Quicklinks" in the API reference section header

### Code Comment Fixes  
- **nemo_curator/backends/xenna/executor.py**: Fixed "initalize" → "initialize" in cleanup comment
- **nemo_curator/backends/experimental/ray_data/executor.py**: Fixed "initalize" → "initialize" in cleanup comment

## Methodology

The typo detection process included:

1. **Spell checking with aspell** on all Markdown and reStructuredText documentation files
2. **Pattern-based search** for 30+ common typo patterns across Python, Markdown, YAML, and configuration files  
3. **Manual review** of critical documentation files (README, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING)
4. **Code analysis** of comments, docstrings, log messages, and error strings
5. **Validation** that all changes maintain code functionality and pass linting

## Validation

- All modified Python files pass compilation and Ruff linting
- Changes are limited to text corrections with no functional impact
- Git diff shows minimal, targeted modifications (3 insertions, 3 deletions)

The comprehensive search revealed that the codebase is generally well-maintained with minimal spelling issues. Most spell checker flags were false positives from technical terminology, proper nouns, and domain-specific vocabulary.

Fixes #979.

pullRequest.title: ✨ Set up comprehensive Copilot instructions with complete API design architecture and framework overview for Python 3.12 and CUDA 12
pullRequest.body:
This PR implements comprehensive GitHub Copilot instructions for the NVIDIA NeMo Curator repository, incorporating the complete API design architecture, framework overview, and task-centric patterns for Python 3.12 with optional CUDA 12 GPU environments.

## Key Features

**Complete API Design Architecture:**
- [x] Task-Centric Architecture: Fault tolerance requirements for Xenna preemption handling with comprehensive design principles
- [x] Map-style (Data-Parallel) Execution: Flexible input/output task mappings allowing `X` to `Y` transformations where both can be `Task | list[Task] | None`
- [x] Core Component Interfaces: Complete specifications for `Task`, `ProcessingStage`, `Pipeline`, and `BaseExecutor` with proper Generic typing
- [x] Resource Specification: GPU memory allocation, NVDEC/NVENC support, and multi-GPU resource management
- [x] Backend Implementations: Xenna and Ray Data executors with adapter pattern for backend translation

**Framework Overview:**
- [x] 📦 Key Components: Detailed documentation of Tasks, Stages, Backends/Executors, and Pipeline components with specific directory structures
- [x] 🔄 Execution Flow: Complete 7-step process from pipeline definition to result collection
- [x] 🎯 Enhanced Design Principles: Backend agnostic, type safety, scalability, extensibility, performance, and composability
- [x] 💡 Multi-Backend Usage Patterns: Examples showing execution on different Ray backends
- [x] 🔧 Development Guidelines: Specific guidance for task types, stage implementation, backend development, and testing

**Development Environment Setup:**
- [x] Python 3.12 as the recommended and tested version (supports 3.10-3.12, excludes 3.11 due to RAPIDS compatibility)
- [x] CUDA 12.x setup for optional GPU acceleration with proper resource specification patterns
- [x] Complete `uv` package manager workflow with dependency groups and virtual environment management
- [x] Technology Stack Documentation: PyTorch, Ray, Transformers, RAPIDS with modality-specific libraries

**Code Quality and Documentation:**
- [x] Markdown Linting Compliance: Fixed all MD022, MD032, MD031, and MD040 formatting issues
- [x] Design Principles: Task-centric architecture with fault tolerance and map-style execution patterns
- [x] Testing Framework: GPU testing support with pytest markers and resource detection
- [x] Code Quality Standards: Ruff linting configuration and type hint requirements
- [x] File Structure Conventions: Modality-based organization with complete component directory mapping

**Recent Updates:**
- [x] Added PyTorch to text processing libraries section
- [x] Removed unsupported GPU fallback patterns 
- [x] Updated pipeline examples to follow API design patterns
- [x] Added comprehensive API design information from api-design.md
- [x] Removed all Dask references as requested
- [x] Updated task-centric architecture description to mention Ray foundation
- [x] Added framework overview with key components and execution flow
- [x] Enhanced design principles with backend agnostic capabilities
- [x] Added development guidelines for different component types
- [x] Updated file structure with complete component directory mapping
- [x] **Fixed all markdown linting issues**: Addressed MD022 (headings spacing), MD032 (lists spacing), MD031 (code blocks spacing), and MD040 (code language specification) for full markdownlint compliance

The instructions provide GitHub Copilot with comprehensive context about the repository's task-based architecture and multi-backend framework capabilities, enabling better code suggestions that align with the fault-tolerant, resource-aware design patterns required for heterogeneous computing environments.

Fixes #981.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

pullRequest.title: Fix RayStageSpecKeys enum membership check for Python < 3.12 compatibility
pullRequest.body:
Fixes the `RayStageSpecKeys` enum membership check that was failing in Python versions prior to 3.12 with a `TypeError`. String enums don't implement the `__contains__` method until Python 3.12, causing validation code like `if key not in RayStageSpecKeys` to fail.

## Problem Fixed

The `RayStageSpecKeys` enum membership check was failing in Python versions prior to 3.12 with a `TypeError`. String enums don't implement the `__contains__` method until Python 3.12, causing validation code like `if key not in RayStageSpecKeys` to fail.

## Solution Implemented

```python
# Changed from (fails in Python < 3.12):
if key not in RayStageSpecKeys:

# To (works across all Python versions):
if key not in {e.value for e in RayStageSpecKeys}:
```

## Changes Made

- Fixed enum membership check in `nemo_curator/backends/experimental/ray_data/adapter.py`
- Added compatibility test in `tests/backends/experimental/test_utils.py` under `TestRayStageSpecKeys` class
- Fixed ruff linting issues (removed trailing whitespace and added final newline)
- Addressed code review feedback: Import `RayStageSpecKeys` from actual source instead of redefining it in test
- Removed `__main__` block from test file to follow proper pytest format
- Cleaned up test format by removing print statements and keeping only assert statements

## Testing

- Verified the fix handles all valid enum values and correctly rejects invalid keys
- All ruff checks pass
- Test now imports the actual enum used in production code for better accuracy
- Test follows standard pytest format and proper file organization
- Test is properly located under `TestRayStageSpecKeys` class in `tests/backends/experimental/test_utils.py` to maintain one-to-one mapping between source and test files

Fixes #1029.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

pullRequest.title: Fix image curation tutorial batch sizes for typical GPUs
pullRequest.body:
The Getting Started with Image Curation tutorial was instructing users to run with extremely large batch sizes (500 for all stages) that cause out-of-memory errors on typical GPUs with 24-48 GB of VRAM.

## Problem
The tutorial examples used batch sizes that were inconsistent with the script defaults:
- **Tutorial examples**: `--task-batch-size 500`, `--embedding-batch-size 500`, `--aesthetic-batch-size 500`, `--nsfw-batch-size 500`
- **Script defaults**: `--task-batch-size 100`, `--embedding-batch-size 32`, `--aesthetic-batch-size 32`, `--nsfw-batch-size 32`

This caused CUDA OOM errors on common GPU hardware, making the tutorial inaccessible to users without high-end 80GB+ GPUs.

## Solution
Updated the tutorial examples to use conservative batch sizes that work reliably on typical hardware:

### Image Curation Example
```bash
# Before
--task-batch-size 500 \
--embedding-batch-size 500 \
--aesthetic-batch-size 500 \
--nsfw-batch-size 500 \

# After  
--task-batch-size 32 \
--embedding-batch-size 32 \
--aesthetic-batch-size 32 \
--nsfw-batch-size 32 \
```

### Image Deduplication Example
```bash
# Before
--task-batch-size 1000 \
--embedding-batch-size 500 \

# After
--task-batch-size 32 \
--embedding-batch-size 32 \
```

## Additional Improvements
- Added comprehensive documentation note explaining that batch sizes are optimized for 24-48 GB GPUs
- Moved batch size guidance to apply to both workflows (image curation and deduplication)
- Enhanced guidance with specific GPU models (H100, B200, A100) and memory-based tuning recommendations
- Provided guidance for users with high-memory GPUs (80 GB+) who want to use larger batch sizes for better performance
- Added recommendations for lower-memory GPUs (16 GB or less)
- Ensured consistency between tutorial examples and script defaults

The conservative batch size of 32 ensures the tutorial works reliably on common GPU hardware (RTX 4090, A6000, RTX A5000, etc.) while maintaining good performance. The enhanced documentation now provides clear guidance for tuning batch sizes based on available GPU memory across different hardware configurations.

Fixes #1049.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

pullRequest.title: Add large file splitting script
pullRequest.body:
This PR adds a little utility script to split large files (bad for processing) into multiple files with a more reasonable size. Marking as WIP and pushing up early for feedback -- cc @ayushdg @sarahyurick 

For testing locally, here's a little snippet to make a large-ish Parquet file (~7 GB in memory):

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

num_rows = 200_000_000
data = {
    "id": np.arange(num_rows),
    "value1": np.random.rand(num_rows),
    "value2": np.random.randint(0, 1000, num_rows),
    "category": np.random.choice(["A", "B", "C", "D"], num_rows),
    "timestamp": pd.to_datetime("2023-01-01") + pd.to_timedelta(np.arange(num_rows), unit="s"),
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.to_parquet("example.parquet")
```

And then you can run the script here like this:

```
python nemo_curator/utils/split_large_file.py --infile example.parquet --outdir foo  --verbose --target-size-mb 128
```

which has output like this

```
Splitting file into smaller ones...

Input file: example.parquet (~7152.56 MB)
Output directory: foo
Target size: 128 MB

Splitting into 64 output files
Saved foo/example_0.parquet with approximate size 111.76 MB
Saved foo/example_1.parquet with approximate size 111.76 MB
Saved foo/example_2.parquet with approximate size 111.76 MB
Saved foo/example_3.parquet with approximate size 111.76 MB
...
...
```

Some room for improvement:

- [ ] Add JSON file support
- [x] Support splitting a directory of large files
- [x] Run in parallel?
- [ ] Support for non-local paths (e.g. using `fsspec` or [`UPath`](https://github.com/fsspec/universal_pathlib))
- [ ] Add support for specifying target size as a string (e.g. "150 MiB"). Definitely not needed, just a small ergonomic thing. 


Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/1156

pullRequest.title: Improve error messages on large file reads
pullRequest.body:
Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/1171.

Summary:

- Add a cheap check for large files in the file partitioning stage
- Follow-up: Since Parquet and JSONL files can be larger in-memory, add a more expensive check for large DataFrames

Additional considerations:

- `TextDuplicatesRemovalWorkflow` uses `FilePartitioningStage` + `JsonlReaderStage`/`ParquetReaderStage` but is not updated by this PR.
- `TextSemanticDeduplicationWorkflow` both use `JsonlReader`/`ParquetReader` but is not updated by this PR.

pullRequest.title: Fixes #1232 reorganize document filter document modifier dir
pullRequest.body:
## Description
<!-- Provide a standalone description of changes in this PR. -->
<!-- Reference any issues closed by this PR with "closes #1234". -->
Fixes: #1232 

Importing `DocumentFilter` eagerly imports many filters, some of which have heavy dependencies - `TokenCountFilter` (transformers; HuggingFace deps), `HistogramFilter` (downloads; cache), and `FastTextLangId` / `FastTextQualityFilter`.

We want to make the `DocumentFilter` import lightweight. Users must opt in for heavier filters explicitly.

Additionally, `DocumentFilter` and specific filters are defined under `nemo_curator/stages/text/filters` while `Filter`/`Score`/`ScoreFilter` are under `nemo_curator/stages/text/modules`.

`DocumentModifier` and other modifiers are under `nemo_curator/stages/text/modifiers` while `Modify` is under `nemo_curator/stages/text/modules`.

This is confusing from a user perspective.

New Directory structures in this PR:

```bash
nemo_curator/stages/text/filters/
├── __init__.py                  # Exposes DocumentFilter, Filter, Score, ScoreFilter
├── doc_filter.py                # DocumentFilter (lightweight, no heavy deps)
├── score_filter.py              # Filter, Score, ScoreFilter
│
├── heuristic/
│   ├── __init__.py              # Exposes string-based heuristic filters directly at the root because they are lightweight
│   ├── string.py                # regex / string-based filters
│   │
│   ├── code/
│   │   ├── __init__.py          # Exposes code-based heuristic filters (logical grouping under heuristic filters)            
│   │   └── code.py              # code-specific heuristic filters
│   │
│   └── repetition/
│       ├── __init__.py          # Exposes repetition detection heuristic filters (logical grouping under heuristic filters)
│       └── repetition.py        # repeated lines / paragraphs / n-grams
│
├── token/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── token_count.py           # TokenCountFilter (transformers; HuggingFace deps)
│
├── histogram/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── histogram.py             # HistogramFilter (downloads; cache)
│
└── fasttext/
    ├── __init__.py
    └── fasttext_filters.py      # FastTextLangId / FastTextQualityFilter
```

```bash
nemo_curator/stages/text/modifiers/
├── __init__.py                 # DocumentModifier, Modify, and lightweight heuristic filters like LineRemover, MarkdownRemover, etc.
├── modifier.py                 # Modify
├── doc_modifier.py             # DocumentModifier
│
├── heuristic/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── c4.py                   # C4-style text normalization
│   ├── line_remover.py         # Line-based removal heuristics
│   ├── markdown_remover.py     # Markdown cleanup / stripping
│   ├── newline_normalizer.py   # Normalize newlines
│   ├── quotation_remover.py    # Remove quoted text
│   ├── slicer.py               # Slice / truncate documents
│   └── url_remover.py          # URL removal heuristics
│
├── fasttext/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── label.py                # FastTextLabelModifier
│
└── unicode/
    ├── __init__.py
    └── reformatter.py          # UnicodeReformatter

```

```bash
nemo_curator/stages/text/modules/ (removed modifier.py and score_filter.py)
├── __init__.py
├── add_id.py
├── joiner.py
└── splitter.py
```



## Usage
<!-- Potentially add a usage example below -->
### Filters
```python
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters import DocumentFilter, Score, Filter, ScoreFilter # lightweight. Only exposes these classes
```

```python
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.heuristic import BoilerPlateStringFilter, LongWordFilter # string-based filters are lightweight. So they are exposed at the root
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.heuristic.code import GeneralCommentToCodeFilter # code-based filters
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.heuristic.repetition import RepeatedLinesFilter # filters detecting repetition
```

```python
# users must opt in for these filters with heavy dependencies. they won't be available directly from text.filters anymore
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.histogram import HistogramFilter
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.token import TokenCountFilter
from nemo_curator.stages.text.filters.fasttext import FastTextLangId, FastTextQualityFilter
```

### Modifiers
```python
from nemo_curator.stages.text.modifiers import DocumentModifier, Modify
```

```python
from nemo_curator.stages.text.modifiers import LineRemover, MarkdownRemover # lightweight. So they are exposed at the root for ease of access
from nemo_curator.stages.text.modifiers.heuristic import LineRemover, MarkdownRemover # can be accessed from here too
```

```python
# users must opt in for these filters they won't be available directly from text.modifiers anymore
from nemo_curator.stages.text.modifiers.fasttext import FastTextLabelModifier
from nemo_curator.stages.text.modifiers.unicode import UnicodeReformatter
```

## Checklist

Note: All commits need to be signed and signed off. This can be done via `-sS` flags while commiting
`git commit -sS -m "...."
-->

    - I tested these: `tests/stages/common/`, `tests/stages/text/modules/`, and `tests/pipelines/`. All of these pass :)
- [x] The documentation is up to date with these changes.

pullRequest.title: Warn and resolve conflict when both blocksize and files_per_partition are specified
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Closes #1401

When both `blocksize` and `files_per_partition` are specified in `FilePartitioningStage`, the stage calls `_get_file_list_with_sizes()` (returning `list[tuple[str, int]]`) but then passes the result to `_partition_by_count()` which expects `list[str]`, causing a confusing `ValueError: Invalid file path or buffer object type: <class 'tuple'>`.

This adds an early check in `__post_init__` that logs a warning and sets `blocksize = None` when both are provided, so `files_per_partition` takes precedence cleanly.

## Usage

```python
# Previously crashed with a cryptic tuple error:
FilePartitioningStage(
    file_paths="/data",
    files_per_partition=1,
    blocksize="128MB",
)
# Now logs: "Both 'files_per_partition' and 'blocksize' were specified.
#            'files_per_partition' will take precedence and 'blocksize' will be ignored."
```

## Checklist

- [x] I am familiar with the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] New or Existing tests cover these changes.
- [x] The documentation is up to date with these changes.

pullRequest.title: Add check to make sure all reserved keywords are attributes and not properties

pullRequest.title: refactor(audio): AudioBatch -> AudioTask single-dict redesign
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Redesigns the audio task model from `AudioBatch` (list-of-dicts) to `AudioTask` (single-dict) and eliminates the `AudioTaskStage` intermediate class — all audio stages now subclass `ProcessingStage[AudioTask, AudioTask]` directly, matching the pattern used by video, text, and image modalities.

### Task model: `AudioBatch` → `AudioTask`

- **Before**: `AudioBatch(Task[list[dict]])` — a batch of manifest entries crammed into one task.  Stages had to loop internally, `num_items` was ambiguous, and `validate_input` couldn't use `hasattr` because `data` was a list.
- **After**: `AudioTask(Task[dict])` — one manifest entry per task.  An `_AttrDict` subclass exposes dict keys as attributes so `base.py`'s `validate_input` works identically to all other modalities.  `num_items` always returns `1`.
- Naming follows the repository convention: `VideoTask`, `FileGroupTask`, `_EmptyTask`, etc.

### Stage hierarchy: direct `ProcessingStage` subclassing

No audio-specific intermediate base class. Each stage subclasses `ProcessingStage[AudioTask, AudioTask]` directly:

| Stage type | Override | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| CPU stages (`GetAudioDurationStage`, `GetPairwiseWerStage`, ALM stages) | `process(task) → AudioTask` | Mutates `task.data` in-place, returns `task` |
| Filtering stages (`PreserveByValueStage`) | `process_batch` only; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` | `process_batch` filters out entries that don't pass |
| GPU stages (`InferenceAsrNemoStage`) | `process_batch(tasks) → list[AudioTask]`; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` | Batched GPU inference with inline validation |
| IO stages (`AudioToDocumentStage`) | `process_batch(tasks) → list[DocumentBatch]`; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` | Aggregates N tasks into one DataFrame |

**Key design decisions:**
- **In-place mutation** — CPU stages mutate `task.data` and return the same `AudioTask` object. ALM stages that replace the entire dict use `task.data.clear()` + `task.data.update(result)` to preserve the `_AttrDict` type.
- **`process()` raises `NotImplementedError` for batch-only stages** — GPU, IO, and filtering stages that only support `process_batch` raise `NotImplementedError` from `process()`, matching the convention used by deduplication stages (`ConnectedComponentsStage`, `KMeansReadFitWriteStage`, etc.).
- **`setup_on_node()` / `setup()` split** — GPU stages (e.g. `InferenceAsrNemoStage`) use `setup_on_node()` to download models to cache (once per node) and `setup()` to load into GPU memory (once per worker). Communication is via filesystem cache, not instance variables.
- **`cache_dir` parameter** — `InferenceAsrNemoStage` accepts an optional `cache_dir` to control where NeMo downloads pretrained model checkpoints.

### Property ordering convention

All stage dataclass fields follow the base class convention: `name` first, then stage-specific parameters, then `resources`, then `batch_size` last.

### Pipeline validation

All stages declare `inputs()` and `outputs()` for pipeline schema validation, including `CreateInitialManifestFleursStage` which declares `outputs() → [filepath_key, text_key]`.

### Configurable backend selection

Both FLEURS (`run.py`) and ALM (`main.py`) YAML tutorial runners support backend selection via Hydra config:

```bash
# Xenna (default)
python run.py --config-path . --config-name pipeline

# Ray Data
python run.py --config-path . --config-name pipeline backend=ray_data
```

Uses a lazy executor registry (`_EXECUTOR_FACTORIES`) with `importlib` to avoid importing unused backends. Import paths updated to `nemo_curator.backends.ray_data` (no longer under `experimental/`).

### Nightly benchmarks

- `audio_fleurs` split into `audio_fleurs_xenna` and `audio_fleurs_raydata` entries
- Added `alm_pipeline_xenna` and `alm_pipeline_ray_data` entries
- Both backends tested in CI for both pipelines

### `ray_stage_spec()` for fan-out stages

- `CreateInitialManifestFleursStage` gains `ray_stage_spec()` returning `{IS_FANOUT_STAGE: True}` so the Ray Data adapter knows this stage produces multiple outputs from a single input.

### Dependencies

- `pynvml` added to the `cuda12` optional dependency group — required by Xenna (`cosmos-xenna`) for GPU detection via NVML. Without it, Xenna silently falls back to "Assuming no GPUs".

Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/1483.

### Documentation

- `nemo_curator/stages/audio/README.md` — comprehensive 1000+ line developer guide covering how to write CPU/GPU stages, `batch_size` configuration, exact call chains, memory characteristics, end-to-end traces for FLEURS and ALM pipelines, and a quick checklist for adding new stages.
- All copyright headers updated to 2026.
- Removed "production"/"experimental" labels from backend descriptions.

### Upstream sync

Merged `NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator` `main` into `audiobatch-redesign` to pick up `ray_stage_spec` support, `ray_data` promotion out of `experimental/`, and other upstream changes.

## Changed files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `nemo_curator/tasks/audio_batch.py` | **Deleted** — replaced by `audio_task.py` |
| `nemo_curator/tasks/audio_task.py` | **New** — `AudioTask(Task[dict])` + `_AttrDict` |
| `nemo_curator/tasks/__init__.py` | Export `AudioTask` instead of `AudioBatch` |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/common.py` | `GetAudioDurationStage` and `PreserveByValueStage` subclass `ProcessingStage` directly; `PreserveByValueStage` overrides `process_batch` for `None` filtering; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/__init__.py` | Updated exports (removed `AudioTaskStage`) |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/inference/asr_nemo.py` | `setup_on_node` downloads model, `setup` loads to GPU; `process_batch` for batched inference; `cache_dir` parameter; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/io/convert.py` | Aggregated `process_batch` for N→1 DataFrame conversion; `process()` raises `NotImplementedError` |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/alm/*.py` | `AudioBatch` → `AudioTask`, direct `ProcessingStage` subclassing, `clear()+update()` in-place mutation |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/metrics/get_wer.py` | `AudioBatch` → `AudioTask`, direct `ProcessingStage` subclassing |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/datasets/fleurs/create_initial_manifest.py` | Added `inputs()`/`outputs()`, unique per-entry `task_id`, property ordering, `ray_stage_spec()` |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/datasets/file_utils.py` | Copyright 2026 |
| `nemo_curator/stages/audio/README.md` | **New** — 1000+ line developer guide |
| `benchmarking/nightly-benchmark.yaml` | Split FLEURS/ALM into xenna + ray_data entries |
| `benchmarking/scripts/audio_fleurs_benchmark.py` | Improved logging and error handling |
| `benchmarking/scripts/alm_pipeline_benchmark.py` | `AudioBatch` → `AudioTask` |
| `benchmarking/ALM_BENCHMARK.md` | Updated benchmark config and local run instructions |
| `tutorials/audio/fleurs/run.py` | Configurable backend (xenna/ray_data) via Hydra |
| `tutorials/audio/fleurs/pipeline.py` | Added `--backend` CLI argument |
| `tutorials/audio/fleurs/pipeline.yaml` | Added `backend: xenna` config key |
| `tutorials/audio/fleurs/README.md` | Backend selection docs |
| `tutorials/audio/alm/main.py` | Configurable backend (xenna/ray_data) via Hydra |
| `tutorials/audio/alm/pipeline.yaml` | Added `backend: xenna` config key |
| `tutorials/audio/alm/README.md` | Backend selection docs, updated test structure |
| `tests/tasks/test_audio_batch.py` | **Deleted** — replaced by `test_audio_task.py` |
| `tests/tasks/test_audio_task.py` | **New** — `AudioTask` + `_AttrDict` tests |
| `tests/stages/audio/**` | Updated for `AudioTask` and `ProcessingStage` patterns |
| `pyproject.toml` | `pynvml` added to `cuda12` optional group |
| `codecov.yml` | Ignore `tutorials/` and `benchmarking/` from patch coverage |

## Test plan

- [x] All 112 audio unit tests pass (22 ASR, 48 ALM, 13 common, 9 WER, 9 FLEURS manifest, 6 convert, 5 AudioTask)
- [x] FLEURS pipeline end-to-end verified (Python script + YAML runner, both xenna and ray_data backends with GPU)
- [x] ALM pipeline end-to-end verified (YAML route, xenna backend)
- [x] FLEURS and ALM benchmarks verified
- [x] Input validation tests cover all stages
- [x] `setup()` / `setup_on_node()` lifecycle tested with mocks for GPU stages
- [x] `cache_dir` parameter tested for both `setup_on_node` and `setup`
- [x] All tutorial README commands verified
- [x] `ruff check` and `ruff format` pass on all modified files
- [x] Copyright headers updated to 2026 across all audio files

pullRequest.title: docs: cross-link Nemotron-CC recipe as a production NeMo Curator example
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds a **Real-World Recipe** callout in `README.md` under the "Why NeMo Curator?" section, linking to the Nemotron-CC pipeline
- Adds a **Recipes** row to the Learn More table in `README.md`
- Adds a new **Production Recipes** section in `tutorials/README.md`
- Closes https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator/issues/1548.

## Context

The [Nemotron-CC curation pipeline](https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Nemotron/tree/main/src/nemotron/recipes/data_curation/nemotron-cc) is a complete end-to-end example of NeMo Curator used at production scale — covering Common Crawl extraction, exact/fuzzy/substring deduplication, ensemble quality classification, and LLM-based synthetic data generation. Cross-linking it here helps users discover a real-world reference implementation of the library.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: Fix Sortformer tutorial issues and add InferenceSortformerStage benchmark
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fix `EnsureMonoStage` to use `ffmpeg` instead of `sox` (not installed in nightly container)
- Fix `InferenceSortformerStage.setup()` model-path resolution bug (`_resolve_model_path`)
- Cap `CallHomeReaderStage` workers to 1 via `xenna_stage_spec` (prevents crash on 64-CPU hosts)
- Update README prerequisites and model limitations
- Add `audio_sortformer_benchmark.py` benchmarking script

Closes #1728
